rows.description: Computus Francisci Webber Rectoris a Secunda Die novembris 1758 ad annum diem novembrum 1759
rows.notes: Page title across the top. Handwriting mostly legible though a few words (middle Latin words and abbreviations) are slightly unclear; no currency columns present on this line.
Clear centered section header text 'Arrearagias recepta'. No amounts on this line; horizontal ruling beneath helps align subsequent columns.

Pounds '34' and shillings '3' are clear. Pence shows a small '3' with a trailing loop that matches the '3/4' fraction symbol on the page; interpreted as 3 and three‑quarters (0.75). Column alignment unambiguous.

Pounds '21' and shillings '12' are legible. Pence position appears blank (no pence figure); chosen as 0. There is slight smudge to the far right that made the pence cell ambiguous but alignment with nearby lines supports this reading.
Clear '20' in pounds column; shillings and pence columns both blank/zero and alignment is unambiguous.

Description legible enough but the right‑hand monetary columns for this row are blank (no figures visible). Could be an item without a recorded amount or part of a grouped entry; placed as entry because it lists a transaction name.
Pounds '13', shillings '13' and pence '11' are all readable though handwriting is slightly compressed; column boundaries clear and values within valid ranges.

rows.description: Little Sew Quixt Keels pro Quadrien
rows.notes: Pounds '10' and pence '6' reasonably clear; shillings column appears as '0'. Handwriting of the description is somewhat uncertain, but numeric columns are aligned. Slight ink fade around pence required judgement.
Pounds '10' and shillings '9' clear; pence '6' visible though a little faint. All values in valid ranges and align with the ruling.
Pounds '25' and shillings '10' clearly written; pence column blank/zero. Description contains an ampersand with following scribble; amount columns well aligned.
This line has no visible monetary figures. Likely a related sub‑item (there is a following short line 'pro Capon') — treated as an entry without amounts.
Short descriptive sub‑line under Wilmot. No amounts visible; appears to be a sub‑item rather than a standalone amount.

Pounds '05' and shillings '12' readable. In the pence cell there is no clear whole pence digit but a small fractional mark (loop) consistent with '3/4'; interpreted as 0 pence and three‑quarters. This is slightly unusual formatting but consistent with other fractional marks on the page.

Pounds '05' and shillings '19' are clear. Pence shows '11' with a small trailing '3/4' style mark similar to other rows; interpreted as 11 and 3/4 (0.75). Slight ink compression next to fraction lowers confidence slightly.
This line mirrors the previous formatting: '05' pounds, '19' shillings, '11' pence with small fractional mark. Alignment with previous line supports identical reading.
Pounds '16' and shillings '18' are legible; pence appears to be '1' (small but distinct). All within valid ranges and aligned with ruling lines.
Clear reading of '02' pounds, '15' shillings and '8' pence. Ink slightly darker here; alignment consistent.
Pounds '11', shillings '17', pence '10' are all clearly written and align well with column guides.
Pounds '01', shillings '16', pence '3' readable though the description contains abbreviated/uncertain words; monetary columns clear.
Pounds '09' and shillings '6' clear; pence '8' legible though slightly cramped. All values valid and aligned.
Pounds '08', shillings '6', pence '8' visible. Handwriting similar to the previous line; confident in numeric reading though description words are abbreviated.

Pounds '01' and shillings '6' clear. Pence column shows no whole pence but a small fractional mark (similar to 3/4 seen elsewhere). Interpreted as 0 pence + 3/4 fraction; reading is faint so confidence moderate.
Large sum '58' pounds is clearly written in the pounds column; shillings and pence blank/zero. Ink heavier here and alignment clear.
Description present but no monetary figures visible for this line. Possibly a heading/sub‑item for subsequent lines.
Pounds '32' and shillings '10' are clearly legible; pence blank/zero. Alignment with the ruling lines is good.
Pounds '03' and shillings '10' clear; pence 0 (blank). No ambiguity in numerals.
Same numeric pattern as previous: '03' pounds, '10' shillings, pence blank. Ink and alignment clear.
Pounds '01' and shillings '15' readable; pence blank/zero. Parenthetical 'mill' visible in description.
Pounds '01' and shillings '8' clear; pence appears as '4' though a little faint. Alignment consistent with other rows.
Pounds '08', shillings '6', pence '8' readable though the description abbreviation is somewhat unclear. Numeric columns align properly.
Pounds absent/zero; shillings clearly '10'; pence blank. The pounds cell shows no figure and is treated as 0.

rows.description: Mendon-ham pro Anno & this part. Anni ad Mic 1758
rows.notes: Pounds '13' and shillings '10' are clearly written; pence blank/zero. Description includes a date reference 'Anni ad Mic 1758' which is legible.
Pounds '04' clear; shillings and pence blank/zero. Description legible and numbers align.
Pounds '04' clearly readable; shillings and pence blank/zero. Handwriting for description is clear.
Pounds '20' clearly visible; shillings and pence blank/zero. The place name is somewhat abbreviated but monetary figures are unambiguous.
Final item before the total shows '0' pounds, '1' shilling and '6' pence clearly in the rightmost columns. Ink is clear here and columns are well aligned.
Final total line at the bottom: clearly written '348' pounds, '19' shillings, '8' pence underlined with a rule. Column alignment is unambiguous and values validate.
